title: Applications Of Linear Systems - Quiz
description: Test your understanding of systems of linear equations in real-world problems

questions.question: A store sells a mixture of coffee beans. Type A costs $8/lb and Type B costs $5/lb. If 10 lbs of the mixture costs $65, which system of equations represents this problem?
questions.explanation: The total weight is 10 lbs (a + b = 10) and the total cost is $65 (8a + 5b = 65). The coefficients 8 and 5 represent the price per pound of each type.

questions.question: In a distance-rate-time problem, a car travels at 60 mph and a truck travels at 45 mph. They start from the same point traveling in opposite directions. After how many hours will they be 315 miles apart?
questions.explanation: When traveling in opposite directions, their distances add up. Using d = rt: 60t + 45t = 315, so 105t = 315, giving t = 3 hours.

questions.question: A supply and demand model has supply equation p = 2q + 10 and demand equation p = -3q + 60. What is the equilibrium quantity?
questions.explanation: At equilibrium, supply equals demand: 2q + 10 = -3q + 60. Adding 3q to both sides: 5q + 10 = 60. Subtracting 10: 5q = 50. Dividing by 5: q = 10.

questions.question: A system of two linear equations has no solution. What does this mean geometrically?

options.text: The lines intersect at exactly one point
The lines are the same line
The lines are parallel and never intersect

questions.explanation: When a system of two linear equations has no solution, the lines are parallel — they have the same slope but different y-intercepts, so they never intersect. This is called an inconsistent system.

questions.question: A chemist needs to mix a 20% acid solution with a 50% acid solution to get 30 liters of a 30% acid solution. How many liters of the 20% solution are needed?
questions.explanation: Let x = liters of 20% solution and y = liters of 50% solution. Then x + y = 30 and 0.20x + 0.50y = 0.30(30) = 9. From the first equation, y = 30 - x. Substituting: 0.20x + 0.50(30 - x) = 9, so 0.20x + 15 - 0.50x = 9, giving -0.30x = -6, thus x = 20 liters.

questions.question: Which method involves solving one equation for one variable and substituting that expression into the other equation?
questions.explanation: The substitution method involves isolating one variable in one equation (e.g., y = 3x + 2) and then substituting that expression into the other equation. This reduces the system to a single equation with one variable.

questions.question: Two trains leave the same station at the same time. Train A heads east at 70 mph and Train B heads west at 80 mph. Which equation gives the time t (in hours) when they are 450 miles apart?
questions.explanation: When two objects travel in opposite directions, the total distance between them equals the sum of their individual distances. Distance = rate × time, so the total distance is 70t + 80t = 150t = 450.

questions.question: In a real-world linear system, what does the intersection point of the supply and demand curves represent?
questions.explanation: In economics, the intersection of supply and demand curves represents the market equilibrium — the price and quantity where the amount suppliers want to sell equals the amount consumers want to buy.

questions.question: A system of equations has infinitely many solutions. What does this mean about the two equations?

options.text: They have different slopes and different y-intercepts
They represent the same line (one is a scalar multiple of the other)
They have the same slope but different y-intercepts

questions.explanation: A system with infinitely many solutions means the two equations represent the same line — they are dependent. One equation can be obtained by multiplying the other by a constant. Every point on the line satisfies both equations.

questions.question: A store sells tickets: adult tickets for $12 and child tickets for $7. If 200 tickets were sold for a total of $1,900, how many adult tickets were sold?
questions.explanation: Let a = adult tickets and c = child tickets. Then a + c = 200 and 12a + 7c = 1900. From the first equation, c = 200 - a. Substituting: 12a + 7(200 - a) = 1900, so 12a + 1400 - 7a = 1900, giving 5a = 500, thus a = 100.
